buy_side_bullets: US economy bullish but hedge the dollar — copper and Brazilian equities as FX hedges. [Druckenmiller]
Big Cycle 'perfect storm': policy recession + eroding foreign confidence + AI arms race; times may resemble pre-1945 era. [Dalio]
Appaloosa +32% H1 on memory chips; 40% cash reflects macro caution while rotating into cyclicals and China. [Tepper]
High-quality stocks 'stupidly cheap' — one of best entry points in years, potential 10x returns. [Ackman]
US equities most expensive ever in Greenlight's history; closing to new investors; owning gold/copper/rates directly. [Einhorn]
sell_side_bullets: BofA Bull & Bear indicator at 8.5 — contrarian sell signal; only 21 stocks making new highs, echoing March 2000 pre-peak. [Hartnett]
Ice Age thesis dead; double-digit inflation now the risk — fiscal dominance, consumer tapped out. [Edwards]
Great Rotation (H1 tech-to-non-tech) then broad H2 selloff on AI capex reversal and mild recession. [Berezin]
S&P could correct to 6,500; inflation won't slow to low-2s% without recession; EPS at $262.50 only 3.3% growth. [Bannister]
AI productivity gains 'years away' — if AI disappoints, unsustainable debt becomes catastrophic; '1999 meets 1990' macro. [Reid]
Three growth forces (AI buildout, industrial renaissance, Big Beautiful Bill) vs. near-term stagflation; S&P 493 earnings at risk. [Slok]

Inflation won't slow to low-2s% without recession; S&P EPS only $262.50 (+3.3%); K-shaped economy unsustainable. [Bannister]
S&P 8,250 target raised post 'gangbusters' Q1 earnings; Roaring 2020s continues; rotation to S&P 493 underway. [Yardeni]
